creator: United States. Collector of Internal Revenue
description: Original certificates of residence issued by the U.S. government in 1894 to Chinese laborers under the provisions of the Act of May 5, 1892 (the "Geary Act"), allowing them to remain and work in the United States. The Geary Act, introduced by California Congressman Thomas J. Geary, and passed by the U.S. Congress on May 5, 1892, extended the Chinese Exclusion Act of 1882. The law required Chinese residents of the United States to apply for a Certificate of Residence and carry it at all times. Failure to do so was punishable by deportation. These original certificates were donated to the City of San Jose in 1965, and are held at History San Jose's Collection Center (Catalog ID 1965-1). Please credit History San Jose when using these images.
